methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Mara operates in Defense & Security as a Pre-Seed company founded by Daniel Kofman and Sriram Raghu, having raised $1.6M to date. The broader sector comprises 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised as a proxy baseline of $300M and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Pre-Seed to subsequent-stage progression, the analysis yields an implied valuation of $750M. The Peer-Median method is not applicable given insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparable data in Defense & Security—while 24 same-sector peers exist, only 1 adjacent-stage comparable was identified, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum needed for reliable peer triangulation.

The triangulated valuation range is $638M to $862M, with a median of $750M. This range is derived from a single primary method, which represents a material limitation. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the $300M total-raised proxy as a valuation anchor for an early-stage company, the generalizability of the 2.5x multiplier to frontier-tech Defense & Security companies, and the absence of direct peer multiples to validate or cross-check the stage-progression result. Additional comparable data or revenue-based metrics would strengthen confidence in this estimate.
